'font-size:.85em;white-space:nowrap;font-family:inherit;flex-shrink:0;\">Undo</button>';\n        s.style.cssText = 'display:none;position:fixed;bottom:28px;left:50%;'\n            + 'transform:translateX(-50%);background:#1e293b;color:#fff;border-radius:12px;'\n            + 'padding:12px 10px 12px 16px;display:none;align-items:center;gap:12px;z-index:9999;'\n            + 'box-shadow:0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,.35);transition:opacity .25s;'\n            + 'min-width:220px;max-width:88vw;';\n        document.body.appendChild(s);\n    }\n})();\nvar _undoTask = null;\nfunction _showUndoSnack(cb, tid, childId, iso, row, isChore) {\n    /* If a previous undo is pending, commit it (hide old row now) */\n    if (_undoTask) {\n        clearTimeout(_undoTask.timer);\n        if (_undoTask.row) { _undoTask.row.style.display = 'none'; }\n        _undoTask = null;\n    }\n    var snack = document.getElementById('undo-snack');\n    if (!snack) return;\n    var lbl = row ? row.querySelector('.dl-label,.dl-sub-label,label') : null;\n    var txt  = lbl ? lbl.textContent.trim().slice(0, 35) : 'Task';\n    var el = document.getElementById('undo-snack-label');\n    if (el) el.textContent = '\\u2713 ' + txt;\n    snack.style.display = 'flex';\n    snack.style.opacity = '1';\n    var timer = setTimeout(function() {\n        /* Time up \u2014 commit: hide the row for real */\n        if (row) {\n            row.style.transition = 'opacity .25s';\n            row.style.opacity    = '0';\n            setTimeout(function() { row.style.display = 'none'; }, 260);\n        }\n        snack.style.opacity = '0';\n        setTimeout(function() { snack.style.display = 'none'; }, 280);\n        _undoTask = null;\n    }, 4500);\n    _undoTask = {cb:cb, tid:tid, childId:childId, iso:iso, row:row, isChore:isChore, timer:timer};\n}\nfunction _undoCheck() {\n    if (!_undoTask) return;\n    clearTimeout(_undoTask.timer);\n    var t = _undoTask; _undoTask = null;\n    /* Restore checkbox and row */\n    t.cb.checked = false;\n    if (t.row) {\n        t.row.setAttribute('data-done', '0');\n        t.row.style.display    = '';\n        t.row.style.opacity    = '1';\n        t.row.style.transition = '';\n        var lbl = t.row.querySelector('.dl-label,.dl-sub-label,label');\n        if (lbl) { lbl.style.opacity = '1'; lbl.style.textDecoration = 'none'; }\n    }\n    var snack = document.getElementById('undo-snack');\n    if (snack) { snack.style.opacity='0'; setTimeout(function(){ snack.style.display='none'; },280); }\n    /* Revert on server */\n    fetch('/toggle-task', {\n        method:'POST',\n        headers:{'Content-Type':'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'},\n        body:'task_id='+encodeURIComponent(t.tid)+'&new_value=false&return_url='\n             +encodeURIComponent('/today?date='+t.iso)\n    }).then(function() { _dashUpdateProgress(t.childId); });\n}\nfunction toggleDashTask(cb, tid, childId, iso) {\n    var row     = document.getElementById('task-' + tid);\n    var isDone  = cb.checked;\n    var newVal  = isDone ? 'true' : 'false';\n    var isChore = row && row.getAttribute('data-chore') === '1';\n    if (row) {\n        row.setAttribute('data-done', isDone ? '1' : '0');\n        var lbl = row.querySelector('label, .dl-sub-label, .dl-label');\n        if (lbl) {\n            lbl.style.opacity        = isDone ? '0.4' : '1';\n            lbl.style.textDecoration = isDone ? 'line-through' : 'none';\n        }\n        if (!isDone) {\n            row.style.display    = '';\n            row.style.opacity    = '1';\n            row.style.transition = '';\n        }\n    }\n    /* On check: show undo snack for 4.5s before hiding the row */\n    if (isDone) { _showUndoSnack(cb, tid, childId, iso, row, isChore); }\n    fetch('/toggle-task', {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: {'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'},\n        body: 'task_id=' + encodeURIComponent(tid) +\n              '&new_value=' + encodeURIComponent(newVal) +\n              '&return_url=' + encodeURIComponent('/today?date=' + iso)\n    }).then(function(r) {\n        if (!r.ok) {\n            cb.checked = !cb.checked;\n            if (row) {\n                row.setAttribute('data-done', isDone ? '0' : '1');\n                row.style.display    = '';\n                row.style.opacity    = '1';\n                row.style.transition = '';\n            }\n        } else {\n            _dashUpdateProgress(childId);\n            if (isDone && !isChore) {\n                setTimeout(function() { _dashAdvance(childId); }, 600);\n            }\n            _dashMaybeHideChoreHeader(childId);\n        }\n    }).catch(function() {\n        cb.checked = !cb.checked;\n        if (row) {\n            row.setAttribute('data-done', isDone ? '0' : '1');\n            row.style.display    = '';\n            row.style.opacity    = '1';\n            row.style.transition = '';\n        }\n    });\n}\n\nfunction _dashAdvance(childId) {\n    /* Show the next hidden-but-pending task in the queue */\n    var queue = document.getElementById('dash-queue-' + childId);\n    if (!queue) return;\n    var tasks = queue.querySelectorAll('.dash-task[data-dash-child=\"' + childId + '\"]');\n    for (var i = 0; i < tasks.length; i++) {\n        var t = tasks[i];\n        if (t.getAttribute('data-done') === '0' && t.getAttribute('data-chore') !== '1'\n                && (t.style.display === 'none' || t.style.opacity === '0')) {\n            t.style.display    = '';\n            t.style.opacity    = '1';\n            t.style.transition = '';\n            break;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nfunction _dashMaybeHideChoreHeader(childId) {\n    var card = document.getElementById('dash-card-' + childId);\n    if (!card) return;\n    var chores  = card.querySelectorAll('.dash-task[data-chore=\"1\"]');\n    var hdr     = document.getElementById('dash-chore-hdr-' + childId);\n    var cntEl   = document.getElementById('dash-chore-cnt-' + childId);\n    var total   = chores.length, done = 0;\n    for (var i = 0; i < chores.length; i++) {\n        if (chores[i].getAttribute('data-done') === '1') done++;\n    }\n    if (cntEl) cntEl.textContent = '(' + done + '/' + total + ')';\n    if (hdr && done === total && total > 0) {\n        hdr.style.transition = 'opacity .3s';\n        hdr.style.opacity    = '0';\n        setTimeout(function() { hdr.style.display = 'none'; }, 320);\n    }\n}\n\nfunction _dashUpdateProgress(childId) {\n    var card = document.getElementById('dash-card-' + childId);\n    if (!card) return;\n    var all  = card.querySelectorAll('.dash-task');\n    var tot  = all.length, done = 0;\n    for (var i = 0; i < all.length; i++) {\n        if (all[i].getAttribute('data-done') === '1') done++;\n    }\n    var pct = tot > 0 ? Math.round(done / tot * 100) : 0;\n    var bar = document.getElementById('dash-bar-' + childId);\n    if (bar) {\n        bar.style.width      = pct + '%';\n        bar.style.background = pct === 100 ? '#22c55e' : pct >= 50 ? '#f59e0b' : bar.style.background;\n    }\n    var cnt = document.getElementById('dash-count-' + childId);\n    if (cnt) cnt.textContent = pct === 100 ? '\\\\u2713 done' : (tot - done) + ' left';\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500 Task Overrides (dismiss / postpone / set time) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nfunction _tovToggle(id) {\n    document.querySelectorAll('.task-ov-panel').forEach(function(p) {\n        if (p.id !== 'tov-' + id) p.style.display = 'none';\n    });\n    var el = document.getElementById('tov-' + id);\n    if (el) el.style.display = el.style.display === 'none' ? 'flex' : 'none';\n}\nfunction _tovClose(id) {\n    var el = document.getElementById('tov-' + id);\n    if (el) el.style.display = 'none';\n}\nvar _TOV_HDR = {'Content-Type':'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'};\nfunction _tovPost(params, cb) {\n    fetch('/task-override', {method:'POST', headers:_TOV_HDR, body:params})\n      .then(cb || function() { location.reload(); });\n}\nfunction _tovAct(tid, child, iso, label, action, postponeTo) {\n    var p = 'task_id='+encodeURIComponent(tid)\n          +'&child='+encodeURIComponent(child)\n          +'&iso='+encodeURIComponent(iso)\n          +'&label='+encodeURIComponent(label)\n          +'&action='+encodeURIComponent(action)\n          +'&json=1';\n    if (postponeTo) p += '&postpone_to='+encodeURIComponent(postponeTo);\n    _tovPost(p);\n}\nfunction _tovActTime(tid, child, iso, label) {\n    var t = document.getElementById('tov-t-' + tid);\n    if (!t || !t.value) return;\n    _tovPost('task_id='+encodeURIComponent(tid)\n           +'&child='+encodeURIComponent(child)\n           +'&iso='+encodeURIComponent(iso)\n           +'&label='+encodeURIComponent(label)\n           +'&action=timed&time='+encodeURIComponent(t.value)+'&json=1');\n}\nfunction _tovClear(tid, child, iso) {\n    _tovPost('task_id='+encodeURIComponent(tid)\n           +'&child='+encodeURIComponent(child)\n           +'&iso='+encodeURIComponent(iso)\n           +'&action=clear&json=1');\n}\n/* \u2500\u2500 Schedule modal (combines Time Today + Move Date + Recurring) \u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nvar _schedCtx = {};\nfunction _schedOpen(tid, child, iso, label) {\n    _schedCtx = {tid:tid, child:child, iso:iso, label:label};\n    var m = document.getElementById('sched-modal');\n    if (!m) return;\n    var lbl = document.getElementById('sched-task-name');\n    if (lbl) lbl.textContent = label;\n    // Pre-fill time input with current time\n    var ti = document.getElementById('sched-time-inp');\n    if (ti) {\n        var n = new Date();\n        ti.value = n.getHours().toString().padStart(2,'0') + ':' + n.getMinutes().toString().padStart(2,'0');\n    }\n    // Pre-fill date input with tomorrow\n    var di = document.getElementById('sched-date-inp');\n    if (di) {\n        try {\n            var base = new Date(iso + 'T00:00:00');\n            base.setDate(base.getDate() + 1);\n            di.min = base.toISOString().split('T')[0];\n            di.value = base.toISOString().split('T')[0];\n        } catch(e) {}\n    }\n    m.classList.add('open');\n}\nfunction _schedClose() {\n    var m = document.getElementById('sched-modal');\n    if (m) No walrus operator (:=)\n    31\u21927. Never use '\\n' inside a JS string within a Python string literal \u2014 use '\\\\n' so the browser receives the escape sequence, not a raw newline\n    32\u21928. multipart/form-data parsing: when fetch POSTs use FormData the server\n    33\u2192   receives multipart/form-data not urlencoded. Always run an in-process smoke test after\n    39\u2192   py_compile to catch NameError, missing variable definitions, and\n    40\u2192   import failures.\n    41\u219210. test fixtures must never write to live data: verification harnesses\n    42\u2192    must always operate on a temp copy of live data files. If a string contains literal\n    48\u2192    ampersands for display use plain ampersands in the source string\n    49\u2192    and let escape() handle it once. This includes render_schedule.py,\n    55\u2192    render_timeblock.py, render_lucy.py, render_lorenzo.py, and any\n    56\u2192    other render file with inline JavaScript.\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192## Data file patterns\n    59\u2192- Most data lives in data/*.json as flat dicts or lists\n    60\u2192- Person keys are title-case in progress.json, chores.json, events.json\n    61\u2192- Person keys are lowercase in auth/pins.json and profiles/ (jp.json not JP.json)\n    62\u2192- Progress keys are compound strings: \"YYYY-MM-DD::Person::task text\"\n    63\u2192- Date keys: YYYY-MM-DD (most), YYYY-Www (meal_plan), YYYY-MM (cycle)\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192## Route patterns\n    66\u2192- GET routes call render_*.py functions that return HTML strings\n    67\u2192- POST routes live in app.py do_POST, chained as elif path == \"/route-name\":\n    68\u2192- JSON POST bodies must be registered in _JSON_PATHS set or the form-parser\n    69\u2192  will consume the payload silently\n    70\u2192- New routes that receive JSON bodies must be added to _JSON_PATHS\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192## Anchor-tag navigation\n    73\u2192Plain `<a href=\"...\">` links cannot POST and cannot mutate server state on\n    74\u2192their own. Any state the destination page needs must either travel in the\n    75\u2192URL query string OR already be persisted before the user clicks. The\n    76\u2192destination handler is responsible for accepting those query params AND\n    77\u2192persisting them on arrival if they are required for subsequent renders.\n    78\u2192Counter-pattern that bit us: the FROL wizard landing buttons are anchors\n    79\u2192to /frol-wizard?step=1&mode=structured. Without persisting `mode` on the\n    80\u2192first GET, the page's \"is the wizard configured?\" gate kept re-rendering\n    81\u2192the landing screen and the wizard appeared unreachable. restore_snapshot | Restore a named snapshot |\n| load_snapshot_data | Load raw snapshot contents |\n| today_iso | Return today's date as ISO string |\n| tomorrow_iso | Return tomorrow's date as ISO string |\n| monday_iso_for | Return the ISO Monday of the week containing `iso` |\n| load_school_week_plan | Return current weekly school plan blob ({} if empty) |\n| save_school_week_plan | Persist the weekly school plan |\n| generate_weekly_school_plan | Generate a draft weekly school plan for JP/Joseph |\n| get_approved_school_week_plan | Return plan only when approved AND for given week |\n| normalize_date_query | Normalize a ?date= query string |\n| safe_int | Coerce to int with default fallback |\n| clean_priority | Validate/clean a task-priority string |\n| clean_status | `get_app_setting`, `get_family_name`, `get_timezone`, `get_schedule_hours`.\n\n---\n\n## Section 7 \u2014 Current claud.md rules (full text)\n\n### Python 3.11 hard rules \u2014 never violate these\n\n1. No backslashes inside f-strings\n2. No nested quotes inside f-strings \u2014 use a variable outside the f-string instead\n3. All POST routing uses elif chains \u2014 never if/elif with a missing first if, never nested if blocks for routing\n4. Never put import statements inside if blocks or functions\n5. All file writes use safe_save_json (tmp file + os.replace) \u2014 never open(f, 'w') directly\n6. No walrus operator (`:=`)\n7. Never use `'\\n'` inside a JS string within a Python string literal \u2014 use `'\\\\n'` so the browser receives the escape sequence, not a raw newline\n8. **multipart/form-data parsing** \u2014 when fetch POSTs use FormData the server receives multipart/form-data not urlencoded. The do_POST handler must sniff Content-Type and parse accordingly using cgi.FieldStorage for multipart. If a POST handler receives empty data check the Content-Type first.\n9. **py_compile passes but runtime fails** \u2014 py_compile only validates syntax not runtime correctness. Always run an in-process smoke test after py_compile to catch NameError, missing variable definitions, and import failures.\n10. **test fixtures must never write to live data** \u2014 verification harnesses must always operate on a temp copy of live data files. Never call save_progress, safe_save_json, or any write helper on live data during testing. Always restore from backup after any test that touches data files.\n11. **double-escaping HTML entities** \u2014 never pass a string that is already HTML-escaped through escape() again. If a string contains literal ampersands for display use plain ampersands in the source string and let escape() handle it once. Strings pre-escaped with `&amp;` will render as visible `&amp;` in the browser if escaped again.\n12. **JS newline in Python f-strings applies everywhere** \u2014 rule 7 applies to ALL files containing JS embedded in Python, not just render_frol_wizard.py. This includes render_schedule.py, render_timeblock.py, render_lucy.py, render_lorenzo.py, and any other render file with inline JavaScript.\n\n### Additional rules (13\u201318)\n\n13. **FROL WIZARD NESTED FORM ADDENDUM** \u2014 The _body_has_form check in _section_chrome looks for `action=\"/frol-wizard\"` in the body string. Any form inside a section body posting to /frol-wizard will suppress the Save and Continue button. Variant tab forms posting to /frol-set-variant are safe. Activity builder forms posting to /frol-add-activity are safe. Before adding any form to a section body confirm its action attribute. This is a recurring bug \u2014 document before fixing if it appears again.\n\n14. **PRE-FLIGHT CHECKLIST** \u2014 Before writing any spec answer these questions. One \u2014 how many files does this touch, list them, if unknown that is a diagnosis step first. Two \u2014 does it involve JavaScript inside Python f-strings, if yes flag the backslash-n rule explicitly in the spec. Three \u2014 does it touch form handling, if yes confirm no nested forms posting to /frol-wizard. Four \u2014 is the root cause confirmed or assumed, if assumed run diagnosis first never draft a fix on an assumed cause. Five \u2014 does it touch multiple files at once, if yes break into separate single-purpose instructions. Six \u2014 does it involve data shape changes or migration, if yes confirm before and after data structure explicitly before writing the spec.\n\n15. **CLAUD.MD READ-BACK REQUIRED** \u2014 At the start of every session read claud.md and paste back every rule found. Then identify which rules apply to today's task. If you cannot paste the rules back accurately stop and ask Lauren to re-paste claud.md before proceeding.\n\n16. **MAGNIFICA HUMANITAS DESIGN PRINCIPLES** \u2014 Every feature built must reflect these principles. One \u2014 the app is a tool not an authority. Every AI suggestion is framed as a suggestion never a prescription. Language uses \"here is one way to think about this\" never \"you should\" or \"the optimal schedule is.\" Two \u2014 companions serve real relationships they do not replace them. Every companion orients toward real people, real community, and real pastoral support. Sister Mary never replaces a confessor. Father Gregory never replaces real mentors. Lucy regularly suggests real conversations over app conversations. Three \u2014 AI supports thinking it does not replace it. Ask before suggesting. Boys build their own plans before seeing AI suggestions. Father Gregory asks questions more than he gives answers. Four \u2014 be transparent about what AI is. Companions never make theological claims with personal authority. Prayer texts come from verified Catholic sources only never AI generated. Five \u2014 language of grace not performance. No gamification no streaks no scores that shame. A hard day is never framed as failure. Sick Day Mode is relief not defeat. Six \u2014 subsidiarity. The family governs itself. Lauren is always the authority. The app serves the family's discernment it does not replace it. Seven \u2014 formation in digital wisdom. Father Gregory teaches the boys to think critically about AI. The explicit goal is that JP finishes high school able to plan his day without the app.\n\n17. **ONE FIX PER INSTRUCTION** \u2014 Never bundle multiple fixes into one Agent instruction unless they are in the same file and directly related. Complex multi-file builds must be broken into sequential single-purpose phases with a compile check and report between each phase.\n\n18. **AUGUST 15TH BUILD PLAN IS THE PRIORITY FILTER** \u2014 Between June 1st and August 15th 2026 every build request must be checked against the August 15th build plan before proceeding. If a requested build is not on the must-have or should-have list for the current week flag it to Lauren before starting. New feature ideas go on the post-September list unless they directly enable one of the 14 goals in the August 15th plan. Scope is the first thing to cut not quality.\n\n### Additional sections of claud.md (not numbered as rules but binding context)\n\n- **What this app is** \u2014 Catholic homeschool family management web app, Python on Replit, single-file HTTP server pattern (app.py + render_*.py), no framework.\n- **Stack** \u2014 Python 3.11; no Flask/Django/FastAPI; JSON files only; plain HTML/CSS/JS rendered as strings; Anthropic via urllib (no SDK).\n- **People** \u2014 Lauren (Mom), John (Dad), JP (14, 9th), Joseph (12, 7th), Michael (5, K), James (13 months, excluded from school/gradebook). Title-case names; Lauren/Mom same person; lowercase keys in auth/pins.json and profiles/.\n- **Data file patterns** \u2014 Most data in data/*.json; person keys title-case in progress/chores/events, lowercase in auth/profiles; progress keys `YYYY-MM-DD::Person::task text`; date keys YYYY-MM-DD (most), YYYY-Www (meal_plan), YYYY-MM (cycle).\n- **Route patterns** \u2014 GET routes call render_*.py functions; POST routes chained as `elif path == \"/route\":` in do_POST; JSON POST bodies must be in `_JSON_PATHS` set or the form-parser eats them.\n- **Anchor-tag navigation** \u2014 Plain `<a href>` can't POST; state must travel in query string OR be persisted before click; destination handler must accept those params AND persist them on arrival. Counter-pattern that bit us: FROL wizard landing anchors `/frol-wizard?step=1&mode=structured`. If a button must trigger persistent state, use `<form method=\"POST\">` styled as a link.\n- **AI calls** \u2014 Model `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`, urllib.request (no SDK), API key from app_settings.json, all responses through `_repair_and_parse_json()`.\n- **Change discipline** \u2014 Additive unless told otherwise; don't modify existing behavior beyond task scope; if a task requires editing out-of-scope files, stop and flag; modules under 800 lines where possible; `render_plan_importer.py` is 1,114 lines (JS lives in `static/js/plan_importer_core.js` / `plan_importer_consult.js`).\n- **FROL Wizard form bypass trap** \u2014 `_section_chrome` suppresses \"Save and Continue\" when `_body_has_form` detects a form with `action=\"/frol-wizard\"`. Utility forms posting to other routes (`/frol-set-variant`, `/frol-add-activity`, `/frol-delete-activity`) are safe. When adding new forms to section bodies, check the action \u2014 if it's `/frol-wizard`, use a different route or handle the advance separately.\n- **Current major features** \u2014 `/plan-import` paste\u2192AI\u2192approve\u2192apply with receipt and per-placement undo; six AI companions (Lucy, Lorenzo, Gregory, Monica, Coach, Izzy); liturgical calendar engine with auto-computed Easter and moveable feasts; per-person daily schedule grids; gradebook with assignment analyzer; meal planner with Lorenzo.\n"}}]}, {"role": "tool", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "File created successfully at: PROJECT_STATE.md"}]}]}

Delivering the four reports. No files were changed.\n\n---\n\n# Lorenzo Fix B \u2014 Diagnosis (4 reports)\n\n## Report 1 \u2014 Where does the meal plan actually get persisted?\n\nThere are **three write candidates**, but only two of them touch *meal data*, and those two write to the **same** store.\n\n| # | Trigger | Function/route | What it writes | Lands in |\n|---|---------|----------------|----------------|----------|\n| A | Editor / fridge UI | **`/meal-save-plan`** (`app.py:10429`) | Reads form fields `week` + `days` (a JSON string), `json.loads` the days, merges each `day\u2192slot` as a `clean_text` plain string into the loaded plan, sets `week`/`start`, calls **`save_meal_plan(plan)`** | `data/meal_plan/{Monday-ISO}.json` |\n| B | Lorenzo chat tags | **`[MEAL_UPDATE]` parser** inside **`/lorenzo-chat`** (`app.py:5806\u20135907`) | Per tag: `load_meal_plan(_planning_week_key())`, mutates one `days[day][slot]` (plain string, or `{\"display\",\"recipe_id\"}` dict for food slots with `|recipe=`, or `pop` to clear), optionally stamps prep/cook/serve + kid-helpers, then **`save_meal_plan(_plan)`** | `data/meal_plan/{Monday-ISO}.json` |\n| C | Guided session cursor | **`save_planning_session`** (`data_helpers.py:2071`) via `start_/advance_/clear_planning_session` | Only session **cursor** state: `{active, week_iso, days, slots, current_day_idx, current_slot_idx, \u2026}` \u2014 **no meal data** | `data/planning_session.json` |\n\nBoth A and B funnel through `render_meals.save_meal_plan()` \u2192 `safe_save_json(_plan_path(key))`, where `_plan_path = data/meal_plan/{week_key}.json` and `key = plan[\"start\"]` (Monday ISO). So **meal content has exactly one store**: `data/meal_plan/{Monday}.json`.\n\n`planning_session.json` is **not** a meal store \u2014 it is a wizard progress pointer. The `[MEAL_UPDATE]` handler only *touches* it afterward by calling `advance_planning_session(day, slot)` to move the cursor (`app.py:5908\u20135917`).\n\n---\n\n## Report 2 \u2014 Which store is authoritative?\n\n**There is a single authoritative meal store, and both readers read it. Nothing is merged at render time.**\n\n- **Fridge card** (`/meal-print` \u2192 `render_meal_print_page`, `app.py:1942`) \u2192 `load_meal_plan(week_key)` \u2192 `data/meal_plan/{Monday}.json`.\n- **Lorenzo chat page** \u2192 `_get_current_meal_plan()` (`render_lorenzo.py:66`) \u2192 `load_meal_plan(_planning_week_key())` \u2192 same `data/meal_plan/{Monday}.json`. It even re-derives `_plan_path(start)` purely to report the file's mtime/\"last saved\" to the model.\n\nThe two are deliberately kept in sync by **`_planning_week_key()`** (`render_meals.py:88`): Mon\u2013Thu \u2192 this week's Monday; Fri\u2013Sun \u2192 next Monday \u2014 explicitly so \"Lorenzo and the print card [don't] disagree about which file is the plan.\" \n\n`_get_planning_session_block()` (`render_lorenzo.py:443`) additionally reads `planning_session.json`, but only to tell the model *which slot to work on next* \u2014 that's cursor state, not meal data, and it isn't part of the rendered fridge card.\n\n**Conclusion:** one authoritative store (`data/meal_plan/{Monday}.json`); `planning_session.json` is an orthogonal cursor. No multi-store merge.\n\n---\n\n## Report 3 \u2014 How does the `[MEAL_UPDATE]` tag channel work?\n\nTags are parsed **out of Lorenzo's Anthropic response text**, server-side, inside the `/lorenzo-chat` POST handler \u2014 *after* the model reply is received (`text` at `app.py:5803`). Parser spine, verbatim (`app.py:5806\u20135907`):\n\n```python\n                # \u2500\u2500 Parse [MEAL_UPDATE:Day:slot[|recipe=ID]]meal[/MEAL_UPDATE] and save \u2500\u2500\n                _meal_rx = _re.compile(\n                    r'\\[MEAL_UPDATE:([^:\\]]+):([^|\\]]+)(?:\\|recipe=([^\\]]+))?\\]'\n                    r'([\\s\\S]*?)\\[\\/MEAL_UPDATE\\]',\n                    _re.IGNORECASE\n                )\n                try:\n                    from render_meals import MEAL_SLOT_SET as _DICT_OK_SLOTS\n                except Exception:\n                    _DICT_OK_SLOTS = {\"breakfast\",\"lunch\",\"dinner\",\"dessert\",\"snacks\",\"dad_lunch\"}\n                _meal_updates_found = []\n                for _mm in _meal_rx.finditer(text):\n                    _mday  = _mm.group(1).strip()\n                    _mslot = _mm.group(2).strip().lower()\n                    _mrid  = (_mm.group(3) or \"\").strip()\n                    if _mrid and not _re.fullmatch(r\"[^\\s\\[\\]]+\", _mrid):\n                        print(f\"[lorenzo MEAL_UPDATE] rejected malformed recipe_id: {_mrid!r}\")\n                        _mrid = \"\"\n                    _mmeal = _mm.group(4).strip()\n                    if _mday and _mslot:\n                        _save_ok = False\n                        try:\n                            _wk = _planning_week_key()\n                            _plan = load_meal_plan(_wk)\n                            if _mmeal:\n                                if _mrid and _mslot in _DICT_OK_SLOTS:\n                                    _plan[\"days\"].setdefault(_mday, {})[_mslot] = {\n                                        \"display\":   _mmeal,\n                                        \"recipe_id\": _mrid,\n                                    }\n                                    # [enrichment block elided for length \u2014 app.py:5847\u20135894:\n                                    #  for recipe-linked dinners, fills prep_time/cook_time from\n                                    #  the recipe card, kid 'helpers' via kid_helpers.assign_kid_steps,\n                                    #  and serve_time from the FROL; \"fill blanks only\".]\n                                else:\n                                    _plan[\"days\"].setdefault(_mday, {})[_mslot] = _mmeal\n                            else:\n                                _plan[\"days\"].setdefault(_mday, {}).pop(_mslot, None)\n                            _plan[\"start\"] = _plan.get(\"start\") or _wk\n                            save_meal_plan(_plan)\n                            _save_ok = True\n                        except Exception as _se:\n                            print(f\"[lorenzo MEAL_UPDATE] save failed for {_mday}/{_mslot}: {_se}\")\n                        if _save_ok:\n                            _meal_updates_found.append((_mday, _mslot))\n```\n\n**Where does it write?** To the **`/meal-save-plan` store** \u2014 i.e. the same `data/meal_plan/{Monday}.json`, via the same `save_meal_plan()`. It does **not** write meal data to `planning_session.json` (it only `advance_planning_session()`s the cursor afterward).\n\n**Is it registered in `_JSON_PATHS`?** **No \u2014 and it doesn't need to be, because it never arrives as a POST body.** `_JSON_PATHS = {\"/plan-import-apply\", \"/plan-import-undo-placement\", \"/curriculum-save\", \"/curriculum-minutes\", \"/poetry-passage-save\"}` (`app.py:3453`). The `[MEAL_UPDATE]` text is extracted from the *LLM response*, not from the request body, so the form-parser-consumes-JSON trap (claud.md) cannot apply to it. \n\nWorth noting for completeness: **`/meal-save-plan` is also not in `_JSON_PATHS`, and that is correct** \u2014 it posts `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` fields (`week=\u2026`, `days=<JSON string>`) and `json.loads` the `days` field itself, so the urlencoded parser handles it fine. The claud.md `_JSON_PATHS` gotcha only bites routes that send a **raw JSON request body**; neither save channel does.\n\n---\n\n## Report 4 \u2014 Recommended architecture\n\n**The hypothesis is half-right.** The *store* is already unified \u2014 both `[MEAL_UPDATE]` and `/meal-save-plan` persist to the same `data/meal_plan/{Monday}.json` through `save_meal_plan()`. So the bug is **not** \"two competing stores.\" The real architectural mismatch is in the **save trigger and the confirmation loop**:\n\n1. **The trigger is brittle free-text regex.** `_meal_rx` requires a perfectly-formed `[MEAL_UPDATE:Day:slot]\u2026[/MEAL_UPDATE]`. Any LLM malformation \u2014 missing closing tag, stray bracket/whitespace, the `|recipe=` id failing `[^\\s\\[\\]]+` \u2014 silently yields no match \u2192 **no save**, with no signal back to the model.\n2. **Narration is decoupled from persistence.** Lorenzo's \"I saved it / I couldn't save it\" prose is generated by the model *in the same reply, before the server runs the save*. `_save_ok` captures the *real* result but only feeds `_meal_updates_found` / the false-confirmation guard \u2014 it never corrects the words the model already wrote. Hence \"narrates success or failure inconsistently\": the words and the disk state are produced by two different actors and never reconciled.\n3. **Why the card still renders anyway:** when a tag *is* well-formed, the save genuinely succeeds to the authoritative store, so the fridge card updates \u2014 independent of whatever Lorenzo said.\n\n**Minimum change that would make saves reliable** (recommendation only \u2014 not implemented):\n\n- **Keep the unified store** (`save_meal_plan` / `data/meal_plan/{Monday}.json`) \u2014 don't re-route to `planning_session.json`; that's correct as-is.\n- **Make the confirmation server-driven, not model-driven.** After parsing, the server already knows `_save_ok` and `_meal_updates_found`. The smallest reliable fix is to **stop trusting the model's prose for save status**: strip any model-authored save claims and have the server **append a deterministic confirmation/\u25b2failure line** built from `_save_ok` (e.g. \"Saved Monday dinner \u2713\" / \"Couldn't save Monday dinner \u2014 tag was malformed, try again\"). This closes the narration\u2194persistence gap without changing where data lands.\n- **Optionally tighten the channel** by treating the structured `/meal-save-plan` route as the single write path and having the chat handler *call that same save logic* with parsed args (so there's one persistence code path, one place backups/validation live), rather than a second inline `load\u2192mutate\u2192save` copy. This is a refactor, not required for correctness, but it removes the duplicate save spine.\n\nNet: the fix is **confirmation integrity** (server reports the truth of `_save_ok`), not store migration. The `[MEAL_UPDATE]` channel can stay, but its success/failure messaging must be emitted by the server from the actual save result, not narrated by the model.\n\n---\n\nNo files were modified. I'm skipping the code-review step because this task produced **zero code changes** \u2014 it's a read-only diagnosis deliverable, so there's nothing to review. Tell me which direction you want for the actual fix and I'll scope it as a single change per Rule 17."}]}]}

 \"\"\"\n    Build a dict describing this child's week:\n      {\n        \"subjects\":  [subj_name, ...],          # ordered union\n        \"day_cells\": {weekday_iso: {subj: status}},\n      }\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        from daily_schedule_engine import load_progress\n        progress = load_progress()\n    except Exception:\n        progress = {}\n\n    # Build ordered subject list across the whole week (curriculum order first)\n    subject_order = {}\n    for _, weekday in days:\n        for subj, _ in _school_subjects_for_day(child, weekday):\n            if subj not in day_cells[iso] = cells\n            continue\n\n        # Past or today: use task_registry to see what was actually planned\n        reg = _registered_school_subjects(child, iso)\n\n        if not reg _item_done(item: dict, progress: dict) -> bool:\n    tid = item.get(\"task_id\", \"\")\n    if tid and tid in progress:\n        val = progress[tid]\n        # progress[tid] may be a bool (legacy) or render_confetti_celebration(child: str) -> str:\n    c_bg   = child_color(child, \"bg\")\n    c_text = child_color(child, \"text\")\n    idx = int(hashlib.md5(child.encode()).hexdigest(), 16) % it up, {escape(child)}!</div>\n        <canvas id=\"confetti-{child}\" style=\"position:absolute;top:0;left:0;width:100%;height:100%;pointer-events:none;\"></canvas>\n    </div>\n    <script>\n    (function(){{\n        var canvas=document.getElementById('confetti-{child}');\n        var ctx=canvas.getContext('2d');\n        canvas.width=canvas.offsetWidth; canvas.height=canvas.offsetHeight;\n        var pieces=[]; var colors=['{c_bg}','#f9ca24','#f0932b','#6ab04c','#e056fd','#22a6b3','#ffffff'];\n        for(var i=0;i<80;i++){{pieces.push({{x:Math.random()*canvas.width,\n pieces.forEach(function(p){{ctx.beginPath();ctx.lineWidth=p.r;ctx.strokeStyle=p.color;\n                ctx.moveTo(p.x+p.tilt+p.r/4,p.y);ctx.lineTo(p.x+p.tilt,p.y+p.tilt+p.r/4);ctx.stroke();}});update();}}\n        function update(){{pieces.forEach(function(p){{p.tiltAngle+=p.tiltSpeed;\n            p.y+=(Math.cos(frame/10)+p.d);p.x+=Math.sin(frame/10)*0.5;\n            p.tilt=Math.sin(p.tiltAngle)*12;\n            if(p.y>canvas.height){{p.y=-10;p.x=Math.random()*canvas.width;}}}});frame++;}}\n        if(frame<300){{setInterval(draw,16);}}\n    }})();\n    </script>\"\"\"\n\n\ndef render_day_nav(base_url: str, iso: str) -> str:\n    try:\n        d = date.fromisoformat(iso)\n    except     if kind == \"task\":\n            # sub_items are carry-over tasks and/or manual tasks\n            for sub in subs:\n                if not isinstance(sub, dict):\n                    continue\n   = round(done_cnt / total * 100) if total else 0\n    all_done   = total > 0 and done_cnt == total\n    bar_col    = \"#22c55e\" if all_done else (\"#f59e0b\" if pct >= 50 else c_bg)\n\n    def title = blk.get(\"label\", \"\")\n        loc   = \"\"\n        if \" \\u2022 \" in title:\n            title, loc = title.split(\" \\u2022 \", 1)\n        loc_html = f' \u00b7 <em>{escape(loc)}</em>'        + _ty_pod_strip(due_thankyou_reminders_for(child), c_bg, \"/today\")\n        + f'</div>'\n    )\n\n\n_DASH_JS = \"\"\"\n<script>\n/* Hide tasks already marked done so they don't reappear as visible checked items on reload */\n(function() {\n    document.querySelectorAll('[data-done=\"1\"]').forEach(function(row) {\n        row.style.display = 'none';\n    });\n    /* Inject undo snackbar once */\n    if (!document.getElementById('undo-snack')) {\n        var s = document.createElement('div');\n        s.id = 'undo-snack';\n        s.innerHTML = '<span id=\"undo-snack-label\" 'transform:translateX(-50%);background:#1e293b;color:#fff;border-radius:12px;'\n            + 'padding:12px 10px 12px 16px;display:none;align-items:center;gap:12px;z-index:9999;'\n            + _showUndoSnack(cb, tid, childId, iso, row, isChore) {\n    /* If a previous undo is pending, commit it (hide old row now) */\n    if (_undoTask) {\n        clearTimeout(_undoTask.timer);\n        if (_undoTask.row) { _undoTask.row.style.display = 'none'; }\n        _undoTask = null;\n    }\n    var snack = document.getElementById('undo-snack');\n    if (!snack) return;\n    var lbl = row ? row.querySelector('.dl-label,.dl-sub-label,label') : null;\n    var txt  = lbl ? lbl.textContent.trim().slice(0, 35) : 'Task';\n    var el = document.getElementById('undo-snack-label');\n    if (el) el.textContent = '\\u2713 ' + txt;\n    snack.style.display = 'flex';\n    snack.style.opacity = '1';\n    var timer = setTimeout(function() {\n        /* Time up \u2014 commit: hide the row for real */\n        if (row) {\n            row.style.transition = 'opacity .25s';\n            row.style.opacity    = '0';\n            setTimeout(function() { row.style.display = 'none'; }, 260);\n        }\n        snack.style.opacity = '0';\n        setTimeout(function() { snack.style.display = 'none'; }, 280);\n        _undoTask = null;\n    }, 4500);\n    _undoTask = {cb:cb, tid:tid, childId:childId, iso:iso, row:row, isChore:isChore, timer:timer};\n}\nfunction _undoCheck() {\n    if (!_undoTask) return;\n    clearTimeout(_undoTask.timer);\n    var t = _undoTask; _undoTask = null;\n    /* Restore checkbox and row */\n    t.cb.checked = false;\n    if (t.row) {\n        t.row.setAttribute('data-done', '0');\n        t.row.style.display    = '';\n        t.row.style.opacity    = '1';\n        t.row.style.transition = '';\n        var lbl = t.row.querySelector('.dl-label,.dl-sub-label,label');\n        if (lbl) { lbl.style.opacity = '1'; lbl.style.textDecoration = 'none'; }\n    }\n    var snack = document.getElementById('undo-snack');\n    if (snack) { snack.style.opacity='0'; setTimeout(function(){ snack.style.display='none'; },280); }\n    /* Revert on server */\n    fetch('/toggle-task', {\n        method:'POST',\n        headers:{'Content-Type':'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'},\n        body:'task_id='+encodeURIComponent(t.tid)+'&new_value=false&return_url='\n             +encodeURIComponent('/today?date='+t.iso)\n    }).then(function() { _dashUpdateProgress(t.childId); });\n}\nfunction toggleDashTask(cb, tid, childId, iso) {\n    var row     = document.getElementById('task-' + tid);\n    var isDone  = cb.checked;\n    var newVal  = isDone ? 'true' : 'false';\n    var isChore = row && row.getAttribute('data-chore') === '1';\n    if (row) {\n        row.setAttribute('data-done', isDone ? '1' : '0');\n        var lbl = row.querySelector('label, .dl-sub-label, .dl-label');\n        if (lbl) {\n            lbl.style.opacity        = isDone ? '0.4' : '1';\n            lbl.style.textDecoration = isDone ? 'line-through' : 'none';\n        }\n        if (!isDone) {\n            row.style.display    = '';\n            row.style.opacity    = '1';\n            row.style.transition = '';\n        }\n    }\n    /* On check: show undo snack for 4.5s before hiding the row */\n    if (isDone) { _showUndoSnack(cb, tid, childId, iso, row, isChore); }\n    fetch('/toggle-task', {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: {'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'},\n        body: 'task_id=' + encodeURIComponent(tid) +\n              '&new_value=' + encodeURIComponent(newVal) +\n              '&return_url=' + encodeURIComponent('/today?date=' + iso)\n    }).then(function(r) {\n        if (!r.ok) {\n            cb.checked = !cb.checked;\n            if (row) {\n                row.setAttribute('data-done', isDone ? '0' : '1');\n                row.style.display    = '';\n                row.style.opacity    = '1';\n                row.style.transition = '';\n            }\n        } else {\n            _dashUpdateProgress(childId);\n            if (isDone && !isChore) {\n                setTimeout(function() { _dashAdvance(childId); }, 600);\n            }\n            _dashMaybeHideChoreHeader(childId);\n        }\n    }).catch(function() {\n        cb.checked = !cb.checked;\n        if (row) {\n            row.setAttribute('data-done', isDone ? '0' : '1');\n            row.style.display    = '';\n            row.style.opacity    = '1';\n            row.style.transition = '';\n        }\n    });\n}\n\nfunction _dashAdvance(childId) {\n    /* Show the next hidden-but-pending task in the queue */\n    var queue = document.getElementById('dash-queue-' + childId);\n    if (!queue) return;\n    var tasks = queue.querySelectorAll('.dash-task[data-dash-child=\"' + childId + '\"]');\n    for (var i = 0; i < tasks.length; i++) {\n        var t = tasks[i];\n        if (t.getAttribute('data-done') === '0' && t.getAttribute('data-chore') !== '1'\n                && (t.style.display === 'none' || t.style.opacity === '0')) {\n            t.style.display    = '';\n            t.style.opacity    = '1';\n            t.style.transition = '';\n            break;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nfunction _dashMaybeHideChoreHeader(childId) {\n    var card = document.getElementById('dash-card-' + childId);\n    if (!card) return;\n    var chores  = card.querySelectorAll('.dash-task[data-chore=\"1\"]');\n    var hdr     = document.getElementById('dash-chore-hdr-' + childId);\n    var cntEl   = document.getElementById('dash-chore-cnt-' + childId);\n    var total   = chores.length, done = 0;\n    for (var i = 0; i < chores.length; i++) {\n        if (chores[i].getAttribute('data-done') === '1') done++;\n    }\n    if (cntEl) cntEl.textContent = '(' + done + '/' + total + ')';\n    if (hdr && done === total && total > 0) {\n        hdr.style.transition = 'opacity .3s';\n        hdr.style.opacity    = '0';\n        setTimeout(function() { hdr.style.display = 'none'; }, 320);\n    }\n}\n\nfunction _dashUpdateProgress(childId) {\n    var card = document.getElementById('dash-card-' + childId);\n    if (!card) return;\n    var all  = card.querySelectorAll('.dash-task');\n    var tot  = all.length, done = 0;\n    for (var i = 0; i < all.length; i++) {\n        if (all[i].getAttribute('data-done') === '1') done++;\n    }\n    var pct = tot > 0 ? Math.round(done / tot * 100) : 0;\n    var bar = document.getElementById('dash-bar-' + childId);\n    if (bar) {\n        bar.style.width      = pct + '%';\n        bar.style.background = pct === 100 ? '#22c55e' : pct >= 50 ? '#f59e0b' : bar.style.background;\n    }\n    var cnt = document.getElementById('dash-count-' + childId);\n    if (cnt) cnt.textContent = pct === 100 ? '\\\\u2713 done' : (tot - done) + ' left';\n}\n\n/* \u2500\u2500 Task Overrides (dismiss / postpone / set time) \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nfunction _tovToggle(id) {\n    document.querySelectorAll('.task-ov-panel').forEach(function(p) {\n        if (p.id !== 'tov-' + id) p.style.display = 'none';\n    });\n    var el = document.getElementById('tov-' + id);\n    if (el) el.style.display = el.style.display === 'none' ? 'flex' : 'none';\n}\nfunction _tovClose(id) {\n    var el = document.getElementById('tov-' + id);\n    if (el) el.style.display = 'none';\n}\nvar _TOV_HDR = {'Content-Type':'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'};\nfunction _tovPost(params, cb) {\n    fetch('/task-override', {method:'POST', headers:_TOV_HDR, body:params})\n      .then(cb || function() { location.reload(); });\n}\nfunction _tovAct(tid, child, iso, label, action, postponeTo) {\n    var p = 'task_id='+encodeURIComponent(tid)\n          +'&child='+encodeURIComponent(child)\n          +'&iso='+encodeURIComponent(iso)\n          +'&label='+encodeURIComponent(label)\n          +'&action='+encodeURIComponent(action)\n          +'&json=1';\n    if (postponeTo) p += '&postpone_to='+encodeURIComponent(postponeTo);\n    _tovPost(p);\n}\nfunction _tovActTime(tid, child, iso, label) {\n    var t = document.getElementById('tov-t-' + tid);\n    if (!t || !t.value) return;\n    _tovPost('task_id='+encodeURIComponent(tid)\n           +'&child='+encodeURIComponent(child)\n           +'&iso='+encodeURIComponent(iso)\n           +'&label='+encodeURIComponent(label)\n           +'&action=timed&time='+encodeURIComponent(t.value)+'&json=1');\n}\nfunction _tovClear(tid, child, iso) {\n    _tovPost('task_id='+encodeURIComponent(tid)\n           +'&child='+encodeURIComponent(child)\n           +'&iso='+encodeURIComponent(iso)\n           +'&action=clear&json=1');\n}\n/* \u2500\u2500 Schedule modal (combines Time Today + Move Date + Recurring) \u2500\u2500\u2500 */\nvar _schedCtx = {};\nfunction _schedOpen(tid, child, iso, label) {\n    _schedCtx = {tid:tid, child:child, iso:iso,
